title: An Emu Has Been Loose in New Jersey for Days. Owner Unknown.
script: A WHAT is loose in Jersey? An emu — a 6-foot, 130-pound flightless bird from Australia — has been roaming Byram Township in Sussex County for several days. First spotted off Waterloo Road. Then in Budd Lake, over a mile away. The Hopatcong Animal Shelter posted the alert: do NOT try to catch it yourself. A team of wildlife handlers went out to attempt a capture. Still no luck. Here's the thing: nobody knows who owns it. No permit. No registration. No panicked farmer calling the cops. Somewhere in northern New Jersey, a person owns a giant Australian bird — and just... hasn't mentioned it. The emu is still out there as of today. So: who exactly lets a 130-pound bird wander New Jersey unsupervised?
